meta.title: Dossier Infrastructure Deployment Research
meta.description: Comprehensive research on alternative domains, industries, and risk areas where the ai-dossier scoring/briefing/digest infrastructure could be deployed beyond AI safety governance.
infrastructure_capabilities: Daily curated digest with scored/prioritized stories
Lead stories with detailed briefings and ticker items
Composite scoring (0-10), priority classification, sentiment analysis, intensity rating
Structured JSON output feeding web UI

categories.name: Existential & Catastrophic Risk Monitoring
categories.description: Tracking low-probability, high-consequence threats to civilization. These areas share the AI safety dossier's core DNA: complex technical topics that demand expert curation for a policy audience.

domains.name: Pandemic & Biosecurity Risk
domains.rationale: Pandemic preparedness is the closest analog to AI safety monitoring. Both involve tracking technical developments (gain-of-function research, novel pathogen detection), regulatory responses (WHO IHR amendments, national biosecurity policy), and emerging threats in real-time. COVID-19 demonstrated catastrophic failure in early-warning information synthesis.
audience: Biosecurity researchers (Johns Hopkins CHS, NTI Bio, Georgetown CSET)
source_types: WHO Disease Outbreak News and PHEIC declarations
Preprint servers (bioRxiv, medRxiv) for novel pathogen research
GISAID and GenBank for genomic surveillance data
National CDC equivalents (US CDC, ECDC, China CDC)
Biosecurity policy journals (Health Security, Biosecurity and Bioterrorism)
domains.value_proposition: Information about pandemic risks is scattered across epidemiological databases, preprint servers, regulatory filings, and policy announcements. A scored daily digest would have been invaluable in January 2020 -- and will be for the next pandemic. The scoring system can flag anomalous outbreak patterns before they reach mainstream media.

market_validation.gap: No public-facing, daily scored digest that synthesizes across technical, policy, and geopolitical dimensions the way the AI dossier does for AI safety.

example_stories.title: H5N1 Mammalian Adaptation: Three Mutations Away From Airborne Transmission
WHO Member States Deadlock on Pandemic Treaty Pathogen Access Provisions

domains.name: Nuclear Risk & Strategic Stability
domains.rationale: Nuclear risk monitoring requires tracking arms control treaty status, modernization programs, geopolitical flashpoints, and technical developments (hypersonics, AI-enabled command and control). The field has a dedicated expert community but lacks a modern, scored daily digest format.
audience: Nuclear security researchers (NTI, SIPRI, FAS, CSIS)
Government officials in foreign affairs and defense
source_types: Federation of American Scientists (FAS) nuclear notebook
IAEA safeguards reports and Board of Governors statements
Defense ministry statements and budget documents
domains.value_proposition: The Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists' Doomsday Clock is annual -- the world needs daily granularity. With New START expiration, modernization programs across all nuclear states, and AI integration into command-and-control, the nuclear risk landscape is more dynamic than at any point since the Cold War.

existing_services: Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists (long-form, not daily digest)
Arms Control Wonk podcast/blog (expert but infrequent)
market_validation.gap: No daily scored intelligence product synthesizing across arms control, geopolitical, and technical dimensions.

domains.name: Climate Tipping Points & Extreme Events
domains.rationale: Climate change operates on long timescales but its policy, economic, and extreme-event manifestations produce daily newsworthy developments. A tipping-points-focused dossier would track both the science (ice sheet dynamics, AMOC stability, permafrost methane) and the cascading socioeconomic consequences.
audience: Climate scientists and Earth system researchers
source_types: Nature Climate Change, Science, PNAS climate publications
IPCC working group reports and special reports
National weather services and NOAA/Copernicus data
Climate litigation databases (Sabin Center, LSE Grantham)
Carbon Tracker and climate finance reports
Insurance industry loss reports (Swiss Re, Munich Re)
Satellite and remote sensing data (ice sheet, deforestation)
domains.value_proposition: Climate risk is increasingly priced into financial markets, insurance, and sovereign debt. A daily scored digest connecting scientific observations to financial and policy implications fills a gap between academic climate science and market-facing risk analysis.

domains.name: Asteroid & Near-Earth Object Monitoring
domains.rationale: Low daily throughput of genuinely newsworthy events limits digest utility. Better suited to alert-based rather than daily digest format. Could work as a sub-section within a broader existential risk dossier.
domains.note: Better as a component of a combined existential risk dossier than standalone.

categories.name: Geopolitical & Democratic Risk
categories.description: Monitoring threats to democratic institutions, electoral integrity, and the global order. This category has massive demand from civil society, journalism, and policy communities.

domains.name: Democratic Backsliding & Institutional Erosion
domains.rationale: Democratic erosion is gradual, multi-dimensional, and hard to track day-to-day precisely because it operates through incremental institutional changes rather than dramatic events. A scored daily digest that flags court-packing, media capture, electoral rule changes, and civil society restrictions across countries would provide unprecedented real-time visibility into democratic health worldwide.
audience: International democracy support organizations (NED, IDEA, NDI, IRI)
Human rights organizations (Freedom House, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty)
source_types: V-Dem Institute democracy indices and alerts
Freedom House Freedom in the World reports and updates
IDEA Global State of Democracy reports
Constitutional court rulings and legislative tracker databases
Committee to Protect Journalists (press freedom incidents)
Civil society organization reports from 100+ countries
domains.value_proposition: V-Dem and Freedom House produce annual or periodic assessments. But democratic erosion happens daily -- a judge removed here, a media license revoked there, an election commission restructured. The daily digest format captures the incremental moves that annual indices miss, providing early warning before a country crosses a threshold.

existing_services: The Economist Intelligence Unit Democracy Index (annual)
market_validation.gap: No daily intelligence product that synthesizes cross-country democratic developments with scoring and prioritization. Demand is enormous from the democracy support and foreign policy communities.

example_stories.title: Georgian Parliament Passes 'Foreign Agent' Law Over Mass Protests
Mexican President Proposes Constitutional Reform to Elect All Judges by Popular Vote

domains.name: Information Warfare & Influence Operations
domains.rationale: State-sponsored disinformation campaigns, coordinated inauthentic behavior, and AI-generated influence operations are accelerating. Tracking attribution, platform responses, and narrative evolution across campaigns requires exactly the kind of multi-source synthesis this infrastructure provides.
audience: Academic researchers (Stanford Internet Observatory, DFRLab, Oxford Internet Institute)
source_types: Platform transparency reports (Meta, X, Google, TikTok)

domains.name: Geopolitical Flashpoints & Conflict Risk
domains.rationale: While general geopolitical intelligence is well-served by existing outlets (Foreign Policy, War on the Rocks, IISS), a scored digest focused specifically on escalation indicators and conflict risk thresholds would be differentiated.
source_types: International Crisis Group reports and CrisisWatch

categories.name: Cybersecurity Threat Intelligence
categories.description: The cybersecurity industry already has a massive threat intelligence market, but most products are either raw indicator feeds or expensive enterprise platforms. A curated, scored daily digest fills a middle-market gap.

domains.name: Cyber Threat Daily Digest
domains.rationale: CISOs and security teams are drowning in alerts and threat feeds. A curated, scored daily digest that synthesizes the most important vulnerabilities, campaigns, and policy developments is exactly what mid-market security teams need. The AI dossier's scoring system maps perfectly to CVSS scores, threat actor severity, and campaign sophistication.
source_types: Vendor security advisories (Microsoft, Google, Apple, etc.)
Threat intelligence reports (Mandiant, CrowdStrike, Recorded Future)
Ransomware leak sites and extortion tracking
domains.value_proposition: Security teams spend hours each morning triaging threat feeds. A pre-scored, pre-prioritized digest with briefings on the 3-4 most critical developments and a ticker of 5-6 additional items saves analyst time and ensures leadership has situational awareness without reading 50 alerts.

existing_services: The Record by Recorded Future (news-style, no scoring)
market_validation.gap: Gap between free news aggregation and six-figure enterprise platforms. A $50-200/month scored daily digest for mid-market security teams.

categories.name: Financial & Economic Systemic Risk
categories.description: Tracking systemic risks to financial stability, supply chains, and macroeconomic resilience. The 2008 crisis and subsequent shocks demonstrated the need for better risk synthesis.

domains.name: Systemic Financial Risk
domains.rationale: Financial stability risks emerge from the interaction of multiple signals: credit spreads, liquidity conditions, regulatory changes, geopolitical shocks, and contagion dynamics. A daily digest synthesizing across these dimensions serves risk managers, regulators, and institutional investors.
source_types: Central bank financial stability reports and minutes
BIS quarterly reviews and working papers
IMF Global Financial Stability Report updates
CDS spreads, VIX, credit market indicators
Bank earnings and capital adequacy reports
Shadow banking and non-bank financial intermediation data
FSOC and European Systemic Risk Board announcements

domains.name: Global Supply Chain Risk
domains.rationale: Post-COVID supply chain disruptions, semiconductor shortages, Red Sea shipping diversions, and critical mineral dependencies have made supply chain intelligence a board-level concern. Daily monitoring of chokepoints, disruptions, and policy shifts (export controls, reshoring incentives) is high-demand.
source_types: Shipping and logistics data (AIS vessel tracking, port congestion)
Natural disaster and climate event monitoring
Labor dispute tracking at key facilities/ports

domains.name: Crypto & DeFi Risk Monitor
domains.rationale: The crypto ecosystem produces daily regulatory actions, protocol exploits, stablecoin depegs, and market structure developments. The 2022 collapse sequence (Terra/Luna -> 3AC -> FTX) showed how contagion propagates. A scored digest focused on systemic risk rather than price action would be differentiated.
audience: Traditional finance institutions with crypto exposure
source_types: DeFi protocol TVL and health metrics (DeFi Llama)

categories.name: Public Health Intelligence
categories.description: Beyond pandemics, public health encompasses ongoing crises that benefit from daily monitoring and expert synthesis.

domains.name: Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR)
domains.rationale: AMR is described as a 'slow-motion pandemic' -- 1.27 million deaths annually, projected to rise to 10 million by 2050. Daily developments in resistance patterns, new antibiotics pipeline, stewardship policy, and agricultural use regulation are poorly synthesized for policymakers.
audience: Global health funders (Wellcome Trust, CARB-X, GARDP)

domains.name: Mental Health & Social Crisis Monitor
domains.rationale: Rising youth mental health crisis, social media impacts, loneliness epidemic, and substance abuse trends. More suited to weekly than daily cadence, but policy developments (Surgeon General advisories, platform regulation, school mental health programs) generate sufficient daily signal.
audience: Health insurance and managed care executives
source_types: State and federal mental health legislation
Platform safety and youth protection announcements

categories.name: Technology & Innovation Tracking (Non-Risk)
categories.description: Moving beyond risk monitoring to opportunity tracking. The same infrastructure that scores threats can score breakthroughs, funding signals, and competitive developments.

domains.name: Quantum Computing & Post-Quantum Transition
domains.rationale: Quantum computing is approaching a critical phase: error correction milestones, the NIST post-quantum cryptography migration deadline, and a startup funding boom. The audience needs daily synthesis of hardware breakthroughs, algorithm advances, standards timelines, and competitive dynamics (US/China/EU).
audience: Venture capitalists and quantum startup ecosystem
Government science and technology policy officials
Pharmaceutical and materials science R&D (quantum applications)
source_types: Nature, Science, Physical Review Letters publications
Quantum hardware company announcements (IBM, Google, IonQ, Quantinuum, PsiQuantum)
Government quantum initiative funding (NQI, EU Quantum Flagship)
Benchmarking data (quantum volume, logical qubit counts)

domains.name: Clean Energy Transition
domains.rationale: The energy transition generates massive daily signal: solar/wind deployment data, battery technology breakthroughs, grid storage milestones, hydrogen economy developments, EV adoption rates, and the policy/subsidy landscape (IRA implementation, EU Green Deal, China industrial policy). High commercial value for investors and industry.
audience: Clean energy investors and project developers
Oil and gas companies managing transition
source_types: IEA and IRENA data and reports
Carbon pricing and emissions trading data
Energy company earnings and capex announcements
domains.value_proposition: BloombergNEF costs $20K+/year. A $100-300/month daily scored digest capturing the same breadth of developments in an accessible format serves the long tail of clean energy professionals, investors, and policymakers.

domains.name: Space Economy & Exploration
domains.rationale: The commercial space industry is booming: launch cadence records, satellite constellation deployments, lunar programs, space debris concerns, spectrum allocation battles, and national space policy. Serves both the industry and the growing space investor community.
source_types: Launch manifests and outcomes (SpaceX, Rocket Lab, etc.)
NASA, ESA, JAXA, CNSA mission updates
Space debris tracking data (18th Space Control Squadron)
Patent filings in propulsion, manufacturing, communications

domains.name: Biotech & Life Sciences Breakthroughs
domains.rationale: CRISPR therapeutics, gene therapy approvals, longevity research, synthetic biology, and the mRNA platform expansion beyond COVID create a rich daily signal. Pharmaceutical and biotech investors need synthesis across clinical trials, regulatory decisions, and basic science breakthroughs.
source_types: FDA/EMA approval decisions and advisory committee meetings
Nature Medicine, NEJM, Lancet, Cell publications
bioRxiv preprints in genomics, synthetic biology
Biotech company pipeline updates and earnings
Patent filings in gene therapy, CRISPR, mRNA
Bioethics journal publications and policy statements

categories.name: Industry-Specific Regulatory & Compliance Intelligence
categories.description: Highly monetizable verticals where professionals need daily monitoring of regulatory changes, enforcement actions, and compliance developments.

domains.name: Pharmaceutical Regulatory Intelligence
domains.rationale: Pharma regulatory affairs teams monitor FDA, EMA, PMDA, and 50+ national agencies daily. Approval decisions, guidance documents, inspection findings, and enforcement actions directly impact billion-dollar product portfolios. Currently served by expensive enterprise platforms (Cortellis, Pharmaprojects) or manual monitoring.
source_types: FDA Federal Register notices, guidance documents, warning letters
Drug safety communications and REMS updates
domains.value_proposition: Regulatory affairs professionals currently spend 2-3 hours daily monitoring agency websites. A scored digest cuts this to 10 minutes. At $200-500/month per seat, with 50,000+ regulatory affairs professionals in the US alone, this is a $100M+ market opportunity.

domains.name: Fintech & Banking Compliance
domains.rationale: The pace of financial regulation is accelerating: open banking mandates, BNPL regulation, crypto compliance (MiCA, SAB 121), AI in lending fairness requirements, sanctions compliance. Compliance teams need daily synthesis.
audience: Legal counsel specializing in financial services
source_types: Federal Reserve, OCC, FDIC, CFPB releases
SEC and CFTC enforcement actions and guidance
European Banking Authority and ECB publications
Industry comment letters on proposed rules

domains.name: Data Privacy & Protection Regulatory Tracker
domains.rationale: With GDPR, US state privacy laws (19+ states), LGPD, PIPL, and dozens of new privacy frameworks worldwide, privacy professionals face a compliance fragmentation nightmare. Daily regulatory intelligence is essential and the audience is large and well-funded.
audience: Chief Privacy Officers and Data Protection Officers
source_types: State and federal privacy legislation tracker
CJEU and national court privacy rulings
Standard contractual clauses and transfer mechanism updates
Children's privacy (COPPA, Age Appropriate Design Code) updates
domains.value_proposition: The IAPP estimates 500,000+ privacy professionals globally. OneTrust and similar platforms focus on compliance tooling, not intelligence. A $100-200/month scored daily privacy digest would serve this audience directly.

categories.name: Social & Cultural Intelligence
categories.description: Tracking societal shifts that don't fit neatly into risk or technology categories but where daily synthesis has high value.

domains.name: Labor Market Transformation
domains.rationale: AI displacement, remote work evolution, gig economy regulation, skills gap data, immigration policy impacts on labor supply, and union organizing trends. Your existing AI dossier already covers the AI-labor angle -- this would expand it to the full labor market picture.
source_types: Indeed, LinkedIn, and Glassdoor hiring data
NLRB filings and union election results
Skills demand data from job postings

domains.name: Education Disruption & EdTech
domains.rationale: AI in education, learning model transformation, credential disruption, student debt policy, and K-12 reform generate moderate daily signal. Higher fit during active policy seasons.

domains.name: Media Ecosystem & Information Landscape
domains.rationale: The collapse of traditional media business models, rise of creator economy, AI-generated content explosion, platform algorithm changes, and journalism sustainability. Meta for understanding how information itself flows.

categories.name: Creative & Unexpected Deployment Areas
categories.description: Domains where a scored daily digest would be novel and potentially viral or high-engagement, even if the risk framing doesn't apply.

domains.name: Legislative & Policy Tracker (Multi-jurisdiction)
domains.rationale: Think of your state AI law tracker in the current dossier -- but applied to ANY policy area. Gun legislation across 50 states. Housing policy. Healthcare reform. Immigration. The infrastructure is perfectly suited to tracking legislative activity across jurisdictions, scoring bills by likelihood of passage, and briefing on the most significant movements.
source_types: Bill text analysis and comparison tools
domains.value_proposition: Current legislative tracking tools (FiscalNote, Quorum) are expensive ($10K+/year) enterprise platforms. A domain-specific scored daily digest at $100-200/month would serve individual lobbyists, small advocacy orgs, and journalists. The template is infinitely replicable: gun policy tracker, housing policy tracker, immigration policy tracker, etc.

market_validation.gap: Massive gap between raw legislative data and expensive enterprise platforms. A scored digest is the missing middle.

domains.name: Scientific Discovery Feed
domains.rationale: A daily digest of the most significant scientific papers across disciplines (or within one discipline), scored by novelty, citation potential, and practical implications. Like a human-curated, opinionated Nature News -- scored and prioritized. Could focus on a specific field (materials science, neuroscience, ecology) or go cross-disciplinary.
source_types: Nature, Science, PNAS, Cell, PRL publications

domains.name: Art Market & Cultural Economy
domains.rationale: Auction results, gallery openings, museum acquisitions, art market trends, cultural policy changes, and the AI art controversy. Niche but passionate audience. Lower daily signal than other domains.

domains.name: Sports Analytics & Transfer Intelligence
domains.rationale: Transfer rumors scored by reliability, injury updates scored by fantasy impact, analytics breakthroughs, sports betting regulatory changes, and league policy developments. High engagement potential. Could be sport-specific (football/soccer transfers, NBA analytics).
domains.note: The quip system from the AI dossier would shine here. Highest engagement potential of any domain.

domains.name: Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) Daily Briefing
domains.rationale: Synthesizing the most significant OSINT findings from satellite imagery analysts, flight trackers, ship trackers, social media monitors, and document leaks. The OSINT community on Twitter/X is massive and active but lacks a curated, scored daily synthesis.
source_types: Corporate registry and beneficial ownership data
Bellingcat and other OSINT organization outputs

domains.name: Longevity & Aging Science
domains.rationale: The longevity field is exploding with funding (Altos Labs, Calico, Unity Biotechnology), clinical trials, and regulatory developments. A passionate community of researchers, investors, and biohackers would consume a daily scored digest. The FDA's evolving stance on aging-as-indication creates unique regulatory tracking needs.

deployment_tiers.description: Recommended prioritization for deploying the infrastructure to new domains, based on fit score, market opportunity, and infrastructure reusability.

domains.name: Pandemic & Biosecurity Risk
domains.reasoning: Closest analog to current AI safety dossier. Existential risk framing, expert audience, policy relevance, massive societal value. The COVID information failure demonstrates clear need.

domains.name: Democratic Backsliding & Institutional Erosion
domains.reasoning: Enormous underserved audience in democracy support community. Daily granularity fills gap left by annual indices. High social impact and philanthropic funding potential.

domains.name: Cybersecurity Threat Intelligence
domains.reasoning: Largest commercial market opportunity. Clear willingness to pay. Gap between free news and $100K+ enterprise platforms. Mid-market security teams are underserved.

domains.name: Legislative & Policy Tracker
domains.reasoning: The dossier's state AI law tracking proves the concept. Infinitely replicable to any policy domain. Gap between raw data and expensive enterprise platforms.

cross_cutting_insights.platform_opportunity: The infrastructure is not just replicable -- it's a platform. The JSON schema, scoring system, tag taxonomy, and lead/ticker structure are domain-agnostic. The primary adaptation required for each new domain is: (1) source configuration, (2) tag taxonomy definition, (3) scoring calibration, and (4) editorial voice/quip style tuning.
cross_cutting_insights.multi_dossier_bundle: A 'risk dashboard' product bundling 3-4 dossiers (e.g., AI + Pandemic + Nuclear + Democratic) would serve the existential risk and global catastrophic risk community directly. Organizations like the Centre for the Study of Existential Risk (CSER), Future of Humanity Institute, and Open Philanthropy already monitor across these domains manually.

monetization_models.description: Free headline + summary, paid full briefings and archive access
Full daily digest with API access to JSON data
Custom scoring, private source integration, team access, Slack/Teams integration
Other products and dashboards consume the scored JSON feed

technical_adaptations_needed: Source plugin architecture -- each domain needs different scrapers/API integrations
Configurable scoring weights per domain (a CVSS-mapped score for cyber vs. a policy-impact score for legislation)
Domain-specific tag taxonomies (could be stored as JSON config per dossier instance)
Tunable editorial voice (cybersecurity audience expects different tone than democracy researchers)
Multi-language support for global domains (democracy backsliding, pandemic surveillance)
